seoTitle: How to Backtest Trading Strategies on TradingView Free
metaDescription: Test your trading strategy on TradingView without a premium plan. Learn to use bar replay and indicators to check win rate and risk-reward before going live.
intro: Backtesting lets you check whether a trading strategy actually holds up before you risk real money on it, and TradingView's free tools make this possible without a paid plan. This guide walks through using the bar replay feature and existing indicators to simulate trades on historical price data. You'll also learn how to read the results - win rate, risk-reward, and accuracy - and avoid the mistakes beginners commonly make while backtesting.

steps.heading: Understand what backtesting actually tests
steps.body: Before running any trades, get clear on what backtesting measures: how a specific strategy would have performed against historical price action.

steps.heading: Use the bar replay tool
steps.body: Turn on TradingView's bar replay feature to step through past price data candle by candle and simulate entries and exits as if you were trading live.

steps.heading: Apply your indicators as you would live
steps.body: Use the same indicators and rules you'd apply in real trading so the backtest results actually reflect how the strategy performs.

steps.heading: Track win rate and risk-reward
steps.body: Log each simulated trade's outcome so you can calculate your win rate and risk-reward ratio across the test period.

steps.heading: Review and avoid common mistakes
steps.body: Check for beginner backtesting mistakes like cherry-picking favorable time periods or ignoring transaction costs, which can make a strategy look better than it really is.

faq.question: Can I backtest for free on TradingView without a paid subscription?
faq.answer: Yes, the bar replay tool along with TradingView's built-in indicators lets you manually backtest strategies without upgrading to a paid plan.

faq.question: Does backtesting guarantee a strategy will work in live trading?
faq.answer: No, backtesting shows how a strategy would have performed historically, but live markets can behave differently, so it's a guide rather than a guarantee.

faq.question: What markets can I backtest on TradingView?
faq.answer: The bar replay and indicator method works across stocks, crypto, and forex, making it useful for intraday, swing, and longer-term traders.

faq.question: What's the most common mistake beginners make when backtesting?
faq.answer: A frequent mistake is only testing on time periods that already favor the strategy, which inflates results compared to how it would perform going forward.
